pointer: "A trial of seven... It came across the narrow sea with the Andals and their seven gods"
note: Aerion demands a trial of seven → Dunk must field seven champions → Baelor joins → Baelor dies in the melee.

pointer: "it was my mace that dealt the fatal blow"
note: Dunk strikes Aerion to protect Tanselle → Dunk is tried → trial of seven → Maekar's mace kills Baelor.

pointer: Aerion maims Tanselle the puppeteer
note: Aerion breaks Tanselle's finger over the slain-dragon puppet -> Dunk attacks Aerion

pointer: Dunk strikes and kicks Prince Aerion
note: Dunk beats Aerion -> Dunk arrested for striking the blood of the dragon

pointer: Aerion demands a trial of seven
note: trial of seven called -> Dunk must find six champions

pointer: no knight will stand for Dunk
note: Dunk's plea unanswered -> Baelor secretly takes his side as the seventh

pointer: the trial of seven is fought
note: melee -> Maekar's mace accidentally kills his brother Baelor

pointer: Baelor dies so a hedge knight might live
note: Baelor's death -> Maekar gives Egg to Dunk as squire

pointer: Bennis cuts the digger Wolmer at the dam
note: Bennis opens Wolmer's cheek → Lady Rohanne demands Bennis or blood → trial by battle

pointer: Wat's Wood burns in the night
note: The wood burns → Eustace confronts the Widow at the stream → trial by battle → Lucas Longinch dies

pointer: Dunk cuts his own cheek "A cheek for a cheek"
note: Dunk pays the blood price in his own blood → Rohanne demands a trial of her innocence over the fire

pointer: "His sons paid for his treason with their life's blood"
note: Eustace's rebellion → royal grant of the Chequy Water to the Webbers → drought-era water war between Standfast and Coldmoat

pointer: Ser Arlan dies of a road-chill
note: Ser Arlan dies -> Dunk inherits his sword, shield, and horses and resolves to enter the Ashford tourney

pointer: Dunk commissions armor from Steely Pate on credit
note: Dunk pays only a two-silver deposit -> he must win a victory to raise the rest of the coin

pointer: Egg rides to Ashford in a lamb-cart
note: Dunk refuses Egg at the inn -> Egg follows to the camp -> Dunk takes him on as squire

pointer: Plummer requires a knight to vouch for Dunk
note: Dunk enters his name as Ser Duncan the Tall → Plummer demands a voucher → Dunk must bring Ser Manfred Dondarrion on the morrow

pointer: Dunk sells Sweetfoot to Henly for 750 silver stags
note: Dunk sells his palfrey → raises coin toward the armor debt owed to Steely Pate

pointer: "He likes to batter men in the training yard, so they will be bruised and vulnerable later"
note: Ser Steffon batters cousin Raymun and baits Dunk → Dunk resolves to hide his skill and enter the lists only on the third day

pointer: "Two of his sons have gone astray on their way here"
note: Maekar's sons Daeron and Aegon missing -> Ser Roland sent to find them

pointer: "By law, only a trueborn son is entitled to inherit a knight's arms"
note: Baelor bars Dunk from Arlan's winged-chalice arms -> Dunk commissions a new device (sunset field, elm, shooting star) from Tanselle

pointer: Ser Manfred Dondarrion refuses to vouch for Dunk
note: Manfred's refusal -> Dunk goes to the Great Hall seeking Plummer -> overhears the princes -> Baelor vouches

pointer: "Aerion's lance took the animal just above the armor... exploded out of the back of his neck"
note: Aerion deliberately kills Ser Humfrey Hardyng's horse -> Humfrey's leg broken in two places -> Humfrey withdraws, four champions remain

pointer: "he's hurting her. The puppet girl"
note: Aerion assaults Tanselle -> Dunk beats Aerion -> Dunk seized by Aerion's men -> Egg reveals identity -> men release Dunk

pointer: "You laid hands upon the blood of the dragon. For that offense, you must be tried"
note: Dunk strikes Aerion -> Dunk arrested and tried -> offered trial by combat -> Aerion demands a trial of seven

pointer: "It must be seven against seven. You must needs find six other knights"
note: Trial of seven decreed -> Dunk must recruit six champions before dawn -> Steffon Fossoway pledges and goes recruiting

pointer: "he told my brother that a huge robber knight... made off with Aegon"
note: Daeron's cover-lie -> Dunk cast as the robber knight -> Maekar takes Dunk as target of his wrath

pointer: "I am the seventh, but for the other side. I fight with Prince Aerion"
note: Ser Steffon Fossoway betrays Dunk and joins the accusers -> Dunk left one champion short

pointer: "ARE THERE NO TRUE KNIGHTS AMONG YOU?"
note: Dunk's pleas to the assembled lords are all refused -> Prince Baelor volunteers as the seventh

pointer: "I will take Ser Duncan's side."
note: Baelor joins Dunk's side -> the trial of seven proceeds seven against seven

pointer: "it was my mace that dealt the fatal blow" -> "the prince did not rise"
note: Maekar's melee mace-blow to Baelor's skull -> Baelor Breakspear dies

pointer: "'I yield,' the dragon whispered" -> "I withdraw my accusation"
note: Dunk beats Aerion into yielding -> Aerion withdraws his accusation and the trial ends

pointer: "he tells me he will serve no knight but you... Will you have him?" -> "My lord father says I am to serve you."
note: Egg insists on Dunk -> Maekar offers a place -> Egg becomes Dunk's squire on the road

pointer: "The water's gone"
note: Coldmoat dams the Chequy Water upstream → the stream runs dry → Dunk rides to investigate

pointer: "Might be this starts with water, but it'll end with blood"
note: Bennis cuts the Coldmoat digger at the dam → the Red Widow will answer → looming Osgrey-Webber violence

pointer: "roust out every able-bodied man of fighting age"
note: Bennis cuts the Red Widow's digger → Eustace musters a peasant levy → Osgrey prepares for war

pointer: "shield-wall drill fails → Dunk tells Eustace the levy will not serve → Eustace proposes the blood price → Dunk agrees to ride to Coldmoat to treat with the Red Widow"
note: within-novella spine of this unit

pointer: "Once we cross the stream, we are in the Widow's power"
note: dam/water dispute → Dunk's embassy into Coldmoat → first meeting with Rohanne

pointer: "You were the one with Bennis at the dam... I ought to hang you"
note: Bennis carves up Wolmer at the dam → Longinch demands Bennis delivered up, threatens Dunk

pointer: "blood price refused → Rohanne demands Bennis be delivered up → ultimatum: bring Bennis by the morrow else fire and sword"
note: negotiation collapse driving the unit forward

pointer: "Dunk appeals in Addam Osgrey's name → Rohanne makes him kneel and slaps him → expels him from Coldmoat"
note: the Addam misstep triggers the slap and expulsion

pointer: "It is her stream. She has a paper" ... "the three-headed dragon of House Targaryen. The king's seal"
note: royal decree proves the Chequy Water is legally Webber's, undercutting Osgrey's claim

pointer: "if Ser Eustace were to die without a male heir of his body, Standfast would revert to the crown"
note: grant's reversion clause makes Standfast contingent on a male heir — the estate's whole legal peril

pointer: "You lied to us... he unfastened his cloak, rolled it up, and put it in the old man's lap"
note: the black-dragon reveal → Dunk resigns Eustace's service

pointer: "they found it guarded by a pair of crossbowmen with spider badges... Dunk could gladly have throttled him"
note: the dam now held by Webber men → Dunk forced to retreat, dispute unresolved

pointer: "When he slew Lancel Lannister, the westermen turned back. Without the king there was no war"
note: the Little Lion parable Eustace presses on Dunk (kill the leader, end the war)

pointer: "Wat's Wood is afire" -> Eustace rides to the ford to confront Rohanne
note: the burning of the wood forces the stream confrontation

pointer: "I demand an apology... or a trial. The choice is his"
note: the parley deadlock -> Rohanne's ultimatum sets up a trial

pointer: septon preaches treason against Bloodraven -> septon beheaded, head on spike at Stoney Sept
note: preaching treason -> execution

pointer: Fiddler invites Dunk to Whitewalls -> Dunk resolves to divert to the wedding feast and lists
note: road meeting draws Dunk toward the tourney

pointer: "Roger of Pennytree... head was smashed in by a mace wielded by a lord with three castles"
note: Gormon Peake killed Ser Arlan's nephew at Redgrass Field (Dunk's grudge seed)

pointer: Butterwell's sons die on both sides at Redgrass Field and his youngest dies -> he makes a new Frey marriage for an heir
note: heirless-ness -> new marriage at Whitewalls

pointer: krakens (Dagon Greyjoy) raid the shores -> Lord Beron Stark gathers swords at Winterfell
note: ironmen raids -> Stark muster (Dunk's stated destination)

pointer: Dunk resolves to see the dragon's egg -> brings the party to Whitewalls and weighs entering the joust
note: desire to see the egg -> attendance at the wedding tourney

pointer: Frey weds his 15-year-old daughter to the 50-year-old widowed Butterwell for grandsons
note: Butterwell's need for an heir -> the Frey marriage feast

pointer: Dunk overhears the plot -> Peake threatens him with death to keep silent
note: overhearing the conspiracy -> Peake's threat against Dunk

pointer: the bride's scullion affair exposed by her little brother -> forced marriage to Lord Butterwell
note: discovery of the affair -> the Butterwell-Frey marriage

pointer: Dunk fears recognition from Ashford -> enters the lists as the Gallows Knight
note: need to hide his identity -> Dunk competes as a mystery knight

pointer: Ser Uthor shifts his lance up at ten yards -> strikes Dunk between the eyes -> Dunk unhorsed and senseless
note: Uthor's raised aim -> Dunk's defeat

pointer: Dunk loses the tilt -> forfeits Thunder, arms, and armor to Ser Uthor
note: defeat -> loss of horse and gear (ruin as a knight)

pointer: Ser Kyle throws his tilt to be recognized -> Caswell does not know him -> Kyle loses his arms, armor, and mount
note: Kyle's staged fall -> his ruin

pointer: the Fiddler unhorses Franklyn Frey -> the Fiddler advances toward the dragon's egg
note: tourney win -> nearer the egg

pointer: "I was paid to strike you there... Six dragons tendered in advance, four more promised when you died"
note: an unnamed patron pays Uthor -> Uthor aims for Dunk's head to kill him

pointer: "Cosgrove is as fond of silver... I shall draw the Old Ox next, then the boy"
note: Uthor bribes the master of the games -> the tourney pairings are fixed

pointer: "He showed the ring to Maester Lothar, who delivered him to Butterwell"
note: Egg tries to use his ring/boot -> he is exposed and taken to Butterwell

pointer: "his dragon's egg vanished, and with it the last dregs of his courage"
note: the egg theft -> Butterwell's nerve collapses

pointer: "They see him as a threat, so they mean for Black Tom to remove him from the Fiddler's path"
note: conspirators fix pairings -> Heddle matched to knock out Uthor

pointer: "There he stands. The whore's son. Seize him"
note: Glendon refuses to throw his match and vows to win the egg -> he is framed and seized for the theft

pointer: "Daemon's mine. I will command his Kingsguard. You are not worthy of a white cloak"
note: Cockshaw's jealous love of Daemon -> he pays Uthor and then tries to murder Dunk

pointer: "the dragon's egg ... was the one he placed there ... I'll wager you it's no more than a painted stone"
note: Peake frames Glendon with a painted stone -> Dunk exposes it -> trial of arms called

pointer: "I shall meet him in the lists, and let the gods determine guilt and innocence"
note: Daemon declares trial of arms -> Glendon Ball to tilt against the Fiddler

pointer: "Ser Glendon Ball wheeled his mare and raised his splintered lance"
note: Glendon unhorses Daemon in the trial of arms -> Glendon acquitted and named the Brown Dragon

pointer: "An army had appeared outside the castle, rising from the morning mists"
note: Bloodraven's host encircles Whitewalls -> Second Blackfyre Rebellion collapses without battle

pointer: "Lord Bloodraven's men surrounded him, pulled him off his horse, and clasped him into golden fetters"
note: rebellion collapses -> Daemon II captured alive -> kept as a court hostage

pointer: "She knelt before the blackberries and began to weep, and he was so moved that he went to comfort her"
note: Rohanne weeps at Addam's grave -> Eustace comforts her -> reconciliation -> marriage

pointer: "his chequy lion flies beside the Webber spider"
note: Dunk kills Lucas in the trial -> Coldmoat and Standfast reconciled -> Eustace weds Rohanne and becomes lord of Coldmoat
